content: Hey Saturday, quick sleep check-in. I started working with my sleep coach, Maya Okonkwo, last Monday (March 3), and my baseline before that was about 5.5 hours a night with 2–3 wakeups.
Great context, thanks for sharing that. Starting from 5.5 hours with 2–3 wakeups gives us a clear baseline to measure progress. Want to walk me through how this week went with Maya’s plan?
Yeah — I followed most of it: no screens 60 min before bed, aiming for 10:30 PM lights out, magnesium glycinate 400mg, and bedroom at 68°F. I hit all 4 on 5 of 7 nights.

That’s a really strong first week, especially getting 5/7 nights with all four interventions. Consistency with the 10:30 PM bedtime and screen cutoff usually drives the biggest early gains. Did you notice any change in total sleep time or number of wakeups yet?
A little better I think — around 6.3 hours average this week, and mostly 1–2 wakeups. The two rough nights were Friday/Saturday when I watched Netflix till late.

Nice progress — going from 5.5 to 6.3 hours with fewer wakeups is meaningful in one week. The weekend pattern makes sense, since late screens can delay melatonin and fragment sleep. Maybe set a hard 9:30 PM Netflix cutoff on Fri/Sat so you still protect the 10:30 target.
Good call. Also, should I keep magnesium glycinate at 400mg nightly, or only on stressful days?

If Maya recommended 400mg nightly, staying consistent for now is usually best so you can judge effects cleanly. You can reassess after 2–3 weeks of stable use and track whether sleep holds up on lower-frequency nights. Keep logging bedtime, wakeups, and total hours so you and Maya can adjust with data.
